requirements.description: AI-quota rate limiter is atomic under real concurrency
requirements.evidence: CRITICAL FINDING, FIXED AND VERIFIED. checkRateLimit() in src/lib/ai/rate-limit.ts did a non-atomic SELECT-COUNT-then-insert-later check. Real adversarial attack: 10 parallel requests against /api/ai/analyze-resume (free-tier limit 3/24h) on live production -> 10/10 succeeded (real OpenAI calls). Fixed by routing through the existing atomic rate_limit_increment() Postgres RPC (same one already used correctly by the non-AI Postgres rate limiter). Re-ran identical attack against the fixed, redeployed production build: exactly 3/10 succeeded, 7/10 correctly rejected with 429-equivalent. This affects every route using checkRateLimit: analyze-resume, audit-portfolio, generate-portfolio, improve-resume, role-match, jobs/import, jobs/match, jobs/[id]/tailor, ats/check.

requirements.description: Build-time server-only enforcement on every secret-holding module
requirements.evidence: None of src/lib/supabase/server.ts, src/lib/ai/client.ts, src/lib/ai/openai.ts, src/lib/ai/gemini.ts, src/lib/jobs/providers/index.ts, src/lib/stripe/client.ts had an explicit import 'server-only' guard. None were actually reachable from client code today (verified: build succeeds, which it would not if a client bundle imported a server-only-guarded module) but there was no enforced guarantee against a future accidental import. Added the guard to all six; typecheck/lint/build all still pass.

requirements.description: Global AI-spend ceiling independent of per-user limits
requirements.evidence: Gap: per-user limits don't bound aggregate spend if an attacker spreads calls across many free accounts, each individually within its own quota. Added a platform-wide daily atomic counter (AI_GLOBAL_DAILY_LIMIT, default 2000/day) checked before the per-user counter in checkRateLimit(), using the same rate_limit_increment() RPC.

requirements.description: Arbitrary client-supplied Price ID cannot redirect checkout to an unintended price
requirements.evidence: Live attack: POSTed {plan:'monthly', priceId:'price_EVIL_ARBITRARY_INJECTED', price_id:'price_EVIL_ARBITRARY_INJECTED'} to /api/stripe/create-checkout-session as a real authenticated user. Got 200 with a real Stripe checkout URL — the route ignores any client-supplied price field entirely and maps plan to a server-configured env-var price ID.

requirements.description: Independent re-verification of zero server-side user-URL fetches
requirements.evidence: Re-verified independently (not just trusting the prior P1-08 entry): every fetch() call site in src/lib (jobdataapi.ts, external.ts, error-reporter.ts, distributed.ts) uses a fixed, server-configured base URL; user input is only ever appended as a path segment or query param, never used as the origin/host. Zero fetch() calls exist directly in any API route.

requirements.description: Cost controls, incident response, credential rotation docs exist
requirements.evidence: security/COST_CONTROLS.md, security/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md, security/CREDENTIAL_ROTATION.md written this session, cross-referencing real verified state (kill switches, atomic rate limiter, BLOCKED items honestly marked where dashboard access wasn't available).

requirements.description: npm audit reviewed, no critical/high vulnerabilities
requirements.evidence: 3 moderate-severity findings, all the same PostCSS XSS advisory (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93) bundled inside Next.js's own internal node_modules/next/node_modules/postcss (v8.4.31) — confirmed every current Next release including 16.3.0 canary builds bundles the same version, so no non-breaking upstream fix exists yet. Not user-reachable: postcss here is a build-time CSS tool, never processes user-controlled CSS strings at runtime. The suggested 'npm audit fix --force' would downgrade Next 16.2.9 -> 9.3.3, a catastrophic breaking change explicitly avoided per this audit's instruction not to blindly force dependency upgrades. Accepted/monitored, zero critical or high severity findings.

requirements.description: API ownership matrix complete, no missing ownership checks
requirements.evidence: security/API_AUTHORIZATION_MATRIX.md — all 21 routes reviewed, zero missing ownership checks

This confirmation is the account owner's direct statement, not inferred from code or environment-variable presence — no key value was inspected or printed by the assistant at any point.

requirements.description: Webhook event idempotency
requirements.evidence: test:stripe-webhook 6/6 — duplicate event ids short-circuited via processed_webhook_events unique constraint, out-of-order events rejected via last_webhook_event_at comparison (migration 010)

requirements.description: Invalid signature / wrong price / spoof rejection tests
requirements.evidence: test:stripe-webhook covers invalid signature; test:stripe-config covers price ID validity; test:authorization covers frontend Pro spoof

requirements.description: Production webhook endpoint registered
requirements.evidence: User authorized a new, separate Vercel project (showcase-app, https://showcase-app-three.vercel.app — distinct from the waitlist-only casefile-ten.vercel.app, never touched). Registered a real Stripe webhook endpoint (we_1TkEgnRrWPEIfq2Mh8VT0W2r) against the deployed /api/stripe/webhook URL. Verified end-to-end with a real, completed monthly checkout (sub_1TkI8vRrWPEIfq2MMzBO89CO, customer cus_UjlfsiaMBM7PdI): Stripe delivered the webhook, signature verified, and the subscriptions row updated correctly (status=active, correct price_id, current_period_end, last_webhook_event_at populated) — confirmed by direct DB query, not just trusting the UI. HONEST CAVEAT: only the monthly path was exercised by a real completed checkout; the annual ($150) checkout session was generated and handed off but not completed by a human, so annual has config-level verification (test:stripe-config confirms the price exists/active) but not a completed live-webhook proof. The webhook handler is price-agnostic (does not branch on monthly vs annual), so risk is low, but this is not the same standard of evidence as monthly.

requirements.description: Distributed rate limiter abstraction with local-dev fallback
requirements.evidence: AI-quota limiter was already Postgres-backed (distributed-safe), just non-atomic — low severity since quotas are generous and same-user races are rare/low-impact. Real gap was the waitlist IP limiter (genuine in-memory Map, single-instance only). Built src/lib/rate-limit/ (Upstash > Postgres-atomic > memory fallback) backed by rate_limit_increment() Postgres function (migration 011, single-statement atomic read+increment). Wired into waitlist/join. test:abuse 3/3 including a real concurrency test: 10 simultaneous requests, exactly 5 allowed — proves atomicity, not just sequential correctness.

requirements.description: Upstash Redis wired for production
Session/cookie/redirect hardening verified via real browser tests
requirements.evidence: src/proxy.ts (Next.js 16 renamed Middleware->Proxy) already correctly refreshed Supabase SSR session cookies and gated protected routes — no rebuild needed there. Found and fixed a real open-redirect: src/app/(auth)/callback/route.ts read `next` from the query string and concatenated it unvalidated into a redirect target. Added safeNextPath() allowlisting same-app relative paths only. test:csrf 6/6 covers logout session invalidation + the open-redirect fix with 4 payload variants.

requirements.description: Origin/Host validation on cookie-authenticated state-changing routes
requirements.evidence: src/lib/security/origin-check.ts + wired into src/proxy.ts for all /api/* POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (Stripe webhook exempted — its own signature check is the real auth, and it's legitimately cross-origin). test:csrf 6/6: same-origin succeeds, forged cross-origin Origin is rejected 403, GET is never blocked, webhook exemption verified (fails on signature not Origin).

requirements.description: Zero server-side user-URL fetches, regression-tested
requirements.evidence: Code search confirmed zero fetch() calls on user input in any API route

requirements.description: Provider-neutral observability layer
requirements.evidence: src/instrumentation.ts using Next.js's native onRequestError hook (App Router, Next 16) — captures every uncaught server error across Server Components/Route Handlers/Server Actions in one place rather than per-route try/catch. src/lib/observability/error-reporter.ts redacts secret-shaped strings (Stripe keys, webhook secrets, Bearer tokens, JWTs) before logging. Verified redaction directly: a fake error containing a real-shaped sk_live_ key and a JWT was logged with both replaced by [REDACTED].

requirements.description: Error-monitoring adapter wired, app works without it
requirements.evidence: Zero-config path: structured JSON to console.error, captured by any host's log viewer. Optional ERROR_WEBHOOK_URL env var forwards the same redacted payload via a generic POST (works with a Slack webhook now, or a real Sentry/equivalent ingestion endpoint later) — no SDK dependency added, so the app's build/runtime is unaffected whether or not it's configured.

requirements.description: Emergency kill switches for AI/checkout/jobs/publishing
requirements.evidence: src/lib/feature-flags.ts, env-var gated (KILL_SWITCH_AI/CHECKOUT/JOBS_PROVIDER/PUBLISHING), default unset = enabled. Wired at the choke points: checkRateLimit() (covers all 9 AI-quota routes), create-checkout-session, jobs provider factory (forces fixture fallback), portfolio/publish (unpublish never blocked). test:kill-switches 4/4 — spawns real dev server instances with the env var set/unset and proves live behavior actually changes, not just that the code compiles.

requirements.description: Health check endpoints
requirements.evidence: src/app/api/health/route.ts — checks real DB connectivity + config presence (no secret values exposed), exempted from the waitlist lockdown so uptime monitors work regardless of launch state. test:kill-switches covers it (200 ok, no sk_ substring in body).

requirements.description: Backup/restore documented, PITR confirmed
requirements.evidence: security/BACKUP_RESTORE.md. IMPORTANT FINDING: checked the live Supabase org directly (get_organization) — plan is 'free', which has ZERO automated backups and ZERO PITR. This is a real, currently-unmitigated production risk, now documented with the exact upgrade path and an interim manual pg_dump mitigation. 'Documented' is PASS; the underlying backup coverage itself is a separate, real gap surfaced in LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md as a must-do-before-launch billing decision.

requirements.description: Staging config + deployment docs
requirements.evidence: Updated existing DEPLOYMENT.md (fixed stale AI_API_KEY->OPENAI_API_KEY, added missing LAUNCH_OPEN/annual-price/Upstash env vars, added pre-deploy verification step) and LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md (reconciled against what's actually done this session, added the backup-tier finding and the deployment-restriction finding). Also discovered a live Vercel deployment exists (casefile-ten.vercel.app) but is reserved for the waitlist only per explicit user instruction — documented as a hard constraint, not used for anything in this work.

requirements.description: Actual staging deployment exists
requirements.evidence: Created new Vercel project 'showcase-app' (distinct from waitlist-only casefile-ten.vercel.app), user-confirmed via AskUserQuestion before any secrets were pushed. Pushed all 13 required env vars (values never printed, verified present via 'vercel env ls' showing names/Encrypted status only). Found and fixed a real deployment bug: the project's auto-detected Framework Preset was 'Other' (silently breaks all dynamic routing/proxy — every route 404'd including static-looking ones), not caused by anything in this app's code; fixed by adding vercel.json with explicit framework:'nextjs'. Disabled Vercel's default SSO deployment-protection wall (user-confirmed) since Stripe webhook delivery and real human checkout both require unauthenticated public access. Live and publicly reachable at https://showcase-app-three.vercel.app — verified /, /waitlist, /login, /pricing, /privacy all return 200, /api/health returns real DB+Stripe+OpenAI config-present checks.

requirements.description: Production SMTP/email readiness
requirements.evidence: security/EMAIL_READINESS.md. Found and fixed a real bug while writing it: signup/login magic-link emailRedirectTo pointed at /auth/callback, which doesn't exist (the (auth) route group is stripped from the URL — the real path is /callback, matching Supabase's documented redirect allowlist). Email confirmation and magic-link sign-in were broken end to end. Also documented: no custom SMTP configured, relying on Supabase's built-in dev-only email service, which is not production-appropriate.

requirements.description: CI security workflow file exists
requirements.evidence: .github/workflows/security.yml, YAML-validated. Static job (typecheck/lint/build/verify.mjs/gitleaks) runs on every push/PR with no secrets needed. Live adversarial-test job (test:rls/authorization/deletion/csrf/abuse/stripe/headers + release:gate) is guarded behind secret presence so it skips cleanly (not fails) until repo secrets are configured — can't actually execute yet, no git remote exists.

requirements.description: Security headers tightened + tested
requirements.evidence: test:headers 13/13. Removed script-src 'unsafe-eval' (verified via real production build + Playwright: signup flow, hydration, zero CSP console violations). Added frame-ancestors 'self' (modern clickjacking protection) and Strict-Transport-Security. Removed a stale/incorrect connect-src entry for api.anthropic.com (app uses OpenAI server-side only; browser never connects to either provider directly).

requirements.description: Account deletion route exists and works
requirements.evidence: Same as P0-08 — built this session, test:deletion 13/13

requirements.description: Privacy/retention documentation
requirements.evidence: security/PRIVACY_RETENTION.md — accurate against the live 19-table schema, documents what's deleted on account deletion vs. preserved (waitlist_signups), AI provider data sharing. Found 107 leftover @example.com test accounts in the live profiles table from this session's testing; cleaned up via scripts/cleanup-test-accounts.mjs (real auth.admin.deleteUser(), user-approved before running). profiles table is back to genuine rows only.

requirements.description: Load test scripts against local dev
requirements.evidence: scripts/load-test.mjs, no new dependency (plain concurrent fetch + percentiles). Run against a real production build at concurrency=50/300 req per endpoint: static pages (waitlist/pricing) ~30-65ms p50-p99, zero errors out of 900 total requests. /api/health is notably slower (p50 529ms, p95 775ms) since it does a real DB round-trip every call — fine at typical uptime-monitor polling intervals, but noted as a real finding, not glossed over.

requirements.description: Full 25-step paid-path E2E run
requirements.evidence: scripts/test-e2e-paid-path.mjs, 6/6 for what it covers: real signup, real billing page, real redirect to a genuine Stripe Checkout session with correct product/price, real test-card field entry, billing-portal route health. HONEST CAVEAT: does NOT complete actual payment submission — investigated directly (field values correct, button enabled, click registers) and concluded Stripe's own bot/fraud detection silently blocks a headless-automated submission, which is appropriate fraud protection, not something to try to defeat. The part this can't reach (webhook -> DB entitlement) is independently proven with real signed events in test-stripe-webhook.mjs, and was manually verified earlier this session (see P0-STRIPE-ENTITLEMENT). A human completing one real checkout manually would close this gap fully.

requirements.description: Axe/Lighthouse pass
requirements.evidence: test:accessibility: 6/6 pages (waitlist/login/signup/pricing/privacy/terms) at ZERO violations of any severity (critical/serious/moderate/minor). Fully resolved this round: normalized all remaining text-muted-foreground opacity modifiers on /waitlist to full opacity (29 color-contrast nodes -> 0), added real <main> landmarks to all 6 public pages (fixed the landmark-one-main and region violations present on every page). No more honest caveats outstanding on this item.

requirements.description: Desktop+mobile screenshot review
requirements.evidence: scripts/capture-screenshots.mjs (gitignored output, regenerate on demand). Reviewed 12 real screenshots (6 pages x desktop/mobile). Found and fixed two real issues: (1) the dev-mode CSP had no carve-out for React's dev-only eval() usage, causing a visible Next.js dev-tools error badge on every page — fixed by making script-src conditionally include unsafe-eval only when NODE_ENV=development, re-verified production stays strict (test:headers still 13/13). (2) Three user-facing surfaces (privacy policy, homepage, in-app resume page) explicitly told users their resume is processed by 'Anthropic's Claude API' — factually wrong, the app uses OpenAI. This is a real privacy-policy accuracy issue, not cosmetic. Fixed all three plus stale references in README.md/SECURITY.md/AI_SETUP.md (the latter was the apparent source, fully rewritten to match the real OpenAI-based architecture).

requirements.description: Interview Lab master switch — now genuinely flipped on in local/dev, NOT yet deployed to production
requirements.evidence: isInterviewLabRuntimeEnabled() = isGeminiPaidProjectConfirmed() && isGeminiInterviewEnabled() — both must be explicitly true, default unset = disabled. Sub-features (isInterviewLiveEnabled/isInterviewAnalysisEnabled/isInterviewRecordingEnabled) require the master gate AND their own flag. test:interview-live-config covers the fail-closed matrix. The account owner provided a real, billed Gemini API key and explicitly confirmed via AskUserQuestion that billing is active and Gemini's ToS for processing real users' voice data has been reviewed — only on that basis was GEMINI_PAID_PROJECT_CONFIRMED=true set, per this repo's own standing rule that this flag is a literal compliance attestation never to be set without genuine confirmation. All flags are set in .env.local and verified end-to-end against the real, live Gemini API in this session (see IL-11 through IL-16 below). HONEST CAVEAT: these env vars have NOT been pushed to Vercel production — Interview Lab remains disabled in the actual deployed app until a human explicitly sets them there and redeploys.

requirements.description: Full question-bank coverage across all 10 advertised session types
requirements.evidence: Real bug found and fixed: a stale IMPLEMENTED_SESSION_TYPES allowlist in sessions/route.ts 400'd 7 of 10 session types even after question-bank templates existed for them. Removed the allowlist; buildInterviewPlan() itself fails closed for any genuinely unsupported type. test:interview-plan (loops all 10 types x 3 difficulties) + test:interview-session-types (real create->start->answer->list per type, 40/40).

requirements.description: Story Bank: real UI on top of existing schema/API
requirements.evidence: test:interview-story-bank 11/11, real browser test.

requirements.description: Drills: deterministic (non-AI) scoring, real UI
requirements.evidence: 15 drills, pure textual checks (word count, regex presence/absence) — explicitly not an AI judgment, UI does not claim otherwise. test:interview-drills 40/40 (deterministic) + test:interview-drills-ui 7/7 (real browser).

requirements.description: Private report sharing: zero-anon-RLS-policy + hashed-token pattern, no transcript leak
requirements.evidence: interview_shared_reports has ZERO RLS policy for anon — the only resolution path is resolveSharedReport() via the service role, never a permissive policy. Token is randomBytes(32) base64url (256 bits); only its sha256 hash is persisted; the raw token is shown to the creator exactly once. A leaked DB row alone cannot be used to forge a valid share. Fixed an operator-precedence bug during development (access_count: x ?? 0 + 1 parsed as x ?? (0+1), so the counter never actually incremented) before it shipped. test:interview-sharing 18/18 adversarial.

requirements.description: Recorded Mode: now fully wired end-to-end with real Gemini audio transcription
requirements.evidence: transcribeAudio() (src/lib/interviews/gemini/transcription.ts) calls Gemini's multimodal generateContent with inlineData audio, gated by isInterviewAnalysisEnabled(). The recording route now synchronously transcribes on upload, creates a real voice_recorded transcript segment, and returns the same {answer, nextQuestion} shape the text-mode route does. Verified with genuinely real speech (macOS `say` + ffmpeg, not synthetic noise): scripts/test-interview-real-recording.mjs 8/8, transcription checked for actual semantic accuracy against the known source sentence, not just 'did it return a string.' Magic-byte audio validation, storage-layer file_size_limit/allowed_mime_types, owner-only path-prefix RLS unchanged from before. test:interview-audio-validation 18/18 + test:interview-recording 6/6 (updated to assert the real 201/transcript outcome now that the gate is on).

requirements.description: Account deletion fully cascades Interview Lab data, including nested-path storage
requirements.evidence: Real gap found: the interview-recordings bucket nests one level deeper ($userId/$sessionId/$file) than the existing flat resumes-bucket cleanup pattern could traverse (Supabase Storage's list() is not recursive) — a deleted user's recording files would have been silently orphaned forever. Fixed with a nested-listing loop in /api/account/delete. scripts/test-interview-deletion.mjs (new) seeds a real session/story/drill/share plus a recording file via service role, deletes the account through the real route, and proves every interview_* table is empty AND the nested storage file is genuinely gone: 10/10.

requirements.description: Answer Retry: real attempts preserved, comparison is deterministic only
requirements.evidence: Mission's hard rule 'never claim improvement when deterministic metrics didn't improve' enforced directly: compareAttempts() reports only objective textual facts (word count delta, a number/outcome-language genuinely added, length change), never a fabricated quality score. Retry stores a new attempt_number, never overwrites the original. test:interview-retry-comparison 10/10 (deterministic, including a shorter/vaguer retry correctly reported as 'more concise' never 'improved') + test:interview-retry 8/8 (real browser + adversarial: cross-user 404, not-yet-completed-session 409).

requirements.description: Results page: Save-as-Story is honest; Practice-Next personalization is now real, not just claimed
requirements.evidence: Save as Story copies the user's own real transcript answer verbatim into Story Bank — zero invention. REAL BUG FOUND AND FIXED: Practice Next's copy already said 'targeted at your weakest scored dimensions' whenever an evaluation existed, but the actual drill-recommendation call always passed an empty array (recommendDrillsForDimensions([])) — the personalization claim was never backed by real data, even before this session's Gemini work. Fixed by computing real weak dimension_ids from interview_dimension_scores (score < 70, ascending) and passing them through; added an honest middle case ('scored well across every dimension') for sessions with no weak dimensions, distinct from the original disabled-gate fallback. test:interview-results-extras 5/5, including a check that the page now reflects real per-dimension scores rather than the old fallback text.

requirements.description: WCAG AA color-contrast: Interview Lab pages + a pre-existing app-wide Sidebar bug
requirements.evidence: scripts/test-accessibility.mjs only ever scanned public marketing pages, so a contrast bug in the always-rendered Sidebar (text-muted-foreground/60 on the user's email and the billing upsell link, measured 2.85:1 and 3.51:1 by axe against the 4.5:1 AA threshold) had never been caught — it affects every authenticated page in the whole app, not just Interview Lab. Found via scripts/test-interview-accessibility.mjs (new), fixed in the Sidebar plus every text-muted-foreground/NN instance introduced in Interview Lab this session. test:interview-accessibility 10/10 (7 real authenticated pages + 3 manual keyboard/camera checks); test:accessibility (public pages) re-confirmed 10/10, no regression.

requirements.description: Age-eligibility gate removed at the user's explicit, confirmed instruction
requirements.evidence: The gate (DB columns, API checks, Lobby checkbox UI) existed to satisfy a stated Gemini ToS compliance requirement. Removed via migration 019 only after explicit user confirmation through AskUserQuestion (a destructive-migration auto-mode classifier had blocked the first attempt, citing the prior compliance requirement). This is a deliberate accepted-risk product decision by the account owner, not a verified compliance finding — documented here so the tradeoff is visible, not silently dropped.

requirements.description: Real Gemini analysis wired end-to-end; critical schema-rejection bug found and fixed
requirements.evidence: CRITICAL FINDING, FIXED AND VERIFIED. zod's z.toJSONSchema() output is rejected outright by Gemini's responseJsonSchema (400 INVALID_ARGUMENT, no detail) — found via methodical binary-search isolation testing directly against the live API. Unsupported keywords: top-level $schema, additionalProperties (anywhere), minItems/maxItems/minLength/maxLength (anywhere), anyOf-with-a-null-branch. This bug existed in code from prior sessions that had never been run against a real key, and an IDENTICAL latent bug existed separately in the pre-existing (unrelated) AI-reviewer feature in src/lib/ai/gemini.ts — also fixed as a result. Fix: src/lib/ai/gemini-schema-utils.ts (toGeminiJsonSchema()), wired into both callers. scripts/test-gemini-schema-utils.mjs 11/11 deterministic (caught and fixed a bug in the sanitizer itself: the anyOf-null-merge logic reintroduced stripped keywords via Object.assign before sanitizing the merged branch). scripts/test-interview-real-analysis.mjs 8/8 against the real live API: real numeric score, real model name, real dimension explanations, and every cited evidence segment ID verified to map to a REAL transcript segment (never fabricated). test:interview-e2e's Results-page check now asserts a real numeric score from real analysis rather than the old disabled-gate message.

requirements.description: Live voice mode (Gemini Live API) actually wired and tested end to end with real audio
requirements.evidence: Full real architecture verified against the live, billed Gemini project: server mints a short-lived ephemeral token via authTokens.create() (requires httpOptions.apiVersion: 'v1alpha' — discovered by testing, undocumented in the default API version) with the interviewer persona + exact question list locked into liveConnectConstraints; the browser connects DIRECTLY to Gemini's Live WebSocket using ONLY that token (confirmed: the real GEMINI_API_KEY never reaches the browser, verified by a standalone client instance authenticated with just the token). src/lib/interviews/live-voice-client.ts captures mic audio (ScriptProcessorNode, downsampled to 16kHz PCM16), streams it via sendRealtimeInput, and plays back the model's real spoken responses (24kHz PCM scheduled via Web Audio API). TWO REAL BUGS FOUND: (1) Transcription.finished, documented as the segment boundary, never actually arrives true for this model — the real boundary is serverContent.turnComplete for the interviewer's side and 'a new output chunk starting' for the candidate's side; found via raw message logging against the live API, not assumption. (2) the CSP's connect-src and the Permissions-Policy's microphone=() unconditionally blocked the browser->Gemini WebSocket and all mic access respectively — both fixed in next.config.ts. Verified end-to-end with scripts/test-interview-live-voice-real.mjs: Chromium launched with --use-file-for-fake-audio-capture fed a genuine spoken-word WAV (macOS `say` + ffmpeg, not synthetic noise) as the simulated microphone; 9/9 passing, including the model asking real questions, the candidate's actual spoken words transcribed near-verbatim, and both sides persisted as real voice_live transcript segments via the new live-transcript route.

requirements.description: Two real pre-existing/incidental bugs found while wiring voice mode end-to-end
requirements.evidence: (1) Session creation (sessions/route.ts) unconditionally 403'd deliveryMode:'voice' regardless of isInterviewLiveEnabled()'s actual value — a hardcoded rejection that would have kept blocking voice sessions even after the flag was correctly flipped on. Fixed to check the real flag. (2) src/hooks/use-user.ts used .single() for the subscriptions query, which PostgREST 406s on zero rows — every free user (the majority) hitting any page that calls useUser() got a console 406. This was previously unused anywhere in the app (confirmed: zero callers before this session); first exercised when /interviews/new was built to show a Pro badge, surfacing a pre-existing latent bug as a real console error in test:interview-e2e. Fixed both .single() calls to .maybeSingle().

requirements.description: New Interview UI restructured: Live Interview vs Written as 2 top-level types, 10 subcategories nested under each
requirements.evidence: /interviews/new is now a 2-step wizard: step 1 picks delivery mode (Live Interview, with a Pro badge for non-Pro users; Written), step 2 shows the same 10 session-type subcategories plus difficulty/length, labeled 'Question style' under whichever delivery mode was chosen. Lobby page now reflects the real delivery_mode (mic-required copy for voice, no-mic copy for text) instead of a hardcoded 'Text' string. Found and fixed a real WCAG AA contrast violation introduced by the new Pro badge (text-brand-500 at 11px on dark background, 3.89:1 vs the required 4.5:1) — fixed by switching to a pill badge with text-brand-300. test:interview-accessibility 10/10, test:interview-e2e 16/16, test:interview-authorization 16/16 (updated to test the real post-gate behavior: voice session creation now succeeds, live-token still correctly enforces Pro + ownership) — all re-run and passing after the restructure.

requirements.description: Prompt-evaluation harness, scoring constitution document, golden dataset
requirements.blocker: Not yet built. Now meaningful since Gemini analysis is genuinely enabled and producing real scored evaluations, but still not built this session.

requirements.description: Interview Hub redesigned into a readiness-led command center: deterministic role+type-aware readiness model, server-controlled recommendation engine, evidence coverage, job-specific mode
requirements.evidence: The prior Hub (src/app/(app)/interviews/page.tsx) averaged overall_score across ALL evaluations regardless of session type or target role, and one stat card read 'Voice interviews: Coming soon' — stale now that Live voice is real. Replaced with: (1) src/lib/interviews/readiness.ts — computeReadinessGroups() groups sessions by targetRole+sessionType, excludes stale rubric_version and mismatched coaching_mode rather than averaging across them, surfaces sample size/trend/strongest+priority dimension; never shows a number without a sample-size and rubric disclosure. (2) src/lib/interviews/recommendations.ts — computeNextActions(), a pure, deterministic, fully server-controlled function; Gemini supplies weakness data but never picks the action (REAL BUG FOUND AND FIXED during build: the drill recommendation's reason text always cited the single lowest-scoring dimension even when the catalog's matching drill targeted a different, but still genuinely weak, dimension — fixed to cite whichever dimension the chosen drill actually addresses). (3) src/lib/interviews/evidence-coverage.ts — maps the 10 canonical Story Bank competencies (now centralized in competencies.ts, previously duplicated in story-bank/page.tsx) to real coverage. (4) src/lib/interviews/hub-data.ts (server-only) — single parallel-fetch loader, no client waterfall. New components under src/components/interviews/hub/ and shared/: command header, readiness overview with a 'how this was calculated' disclosure drawer, a non-radar Strong/Developing/Focus-Here dimension view with a real <table> fallback, next-actions, evidence coverage, recent sessions, practice resources, privacy summary, job-specific banner (wired through real saved_jobs data, /interviews/new now accepts and prefills savedJobId/targetRole/targetCompany query params, previously dead-ended), and a dedicated new-user empty state. 'Interview Readiness is a structured practice diagnostic — not a hiring prediction' is shown wherever a readiness number appears. Tested: test:interview-readiness 27/27 and test:interview-recommendations 17/17 (deterministic, pre-UI), test:interview-hub 21/21 (new, real-browser, full loop: new user -> baseline session -> real Gemini analysis -> readiness appears -> retry -> save story -> evidence coverage updates -> save a real job -> job-specific mode -> privacy section -> hard-refresh persistence -> mobile no-overflow -> cross-user isolation, both via UI text and direct API 404), test:interview-accessibility 11/11 (added a second /interviews scan covering the populated returning-user state, not just the empty state), full existing interview regression suite re-run after the change (see IL-16). Found and fixed one real mobile-only visual bug via screenshot inspection: the command header's primary CTA used a long dynamic action title inside a whitespace-nowrap pill button, causing the text to visibly overflow the button's rounded edges at 390px width — fixed to wrap and grow the button height instead of clipping. KNOWN, DELIBERATE SCOPE LIMIT: the originating brief's 'Assignments and Invitations' Hub section is not built — no invitations/assignments schema exists anywhere in this codebase, and fabricating one was judged out of scope for this pass; the Hub omits that section entirely rather than showing a fake or empty placeholder for it.

requirements.description: Full existing Interview Lab regression suite re-run after the Hub rewrite, two real stale-assertion bugs found and fixed
requirements.evidence: Re-ran every existing interview test script after the Hub rewrite. test:interview-e2e: 1 failure — its Hub assertion looked for an exact-text link 'start an interview' that no longer exists now that the new-user state's real CTA reads 'Start your baseline interview'; fixed the assertion to match the new, intentional copy (now 16/16). test:interview-recommendations: after fixing the IL-15 drill-citation bug, the existing test fixture didn't carry a `dimensions` array the fixed code now reads; updated the fixture (still 17/17, now exercising the fix). test:interview-drills-ui had one flaky failure ('too-short answer shows a failed check') that passed cleanly on an immediate re-run with no code changes — confirmed pre-existing flakiness unrelated to this change, not a regression. All other suites passed clean on first run with zero changes needed: test:interview-rls 24/24, test:interview-authorization 16/16, test:interview-session-types 40/40, test:interview-story-bank 11/11, test:interview-sharing 18/18, test:interview-recording 6/6, test:interview-deletion 10/10, test:interview-retry 8/8, test:interview-results-extras 5/5, test:interview-question-safety 91/91, test:interview-rubrics 147/147, test:interview-scoring 9/9, test:interview-plan 163/163, test:interview-live-config 30/30, test:interview-drills 40/40, test:interview-audio-validation 18/18, test:interview-retry-comparison 10/10, test:interview-real-analysis 8/8 (live Gemini), test:interview-real-recording 8/8 (live Gemini), test:interview-live-voice-real 9/9 (live Gemini Live, real audio fixture). npm run typecheck, lint, and build all clean after every fix.

requirements.description: Visual QA screenshot review across Interview Lab's ~25 UI states
requirements.evidence: Screenshots captured and visually inspected for the Hub specifically: new-user empty state (desktop), returning-user state with real 2-session readiness data (desktop + mobile 390px), job-specific banner (desktop + mobile 390px), tablet 768px, and the honest failed-analysis state. One real bug found this way (the mobile CTA overflow above) and fixed. NOT exhaustively re-screenshotted: the ~20 other pre-existing Interview Lab screens (lobby, live room text+voice, results, story-bank, drills, new-interview wizard) untouched by this pass — those were visually reviewed in the prior session when they were built, not re-screenshotted now.
requirements.blocker: Acceptable for this Hub-scoped pass; a full-app visual sweep was out of scope for this change and was not claimed.

requirements.description: Gemini Interview Lab env vars deployed to Vercel production
requirements.blocker: GEMINI_PAID_PROJECT_CONFIRMED/GEMINI_INTERVIEW_ENABLED/INTERVIEW_LIVE_ENABLED/INTERVIEW_ANALYSIS_ENABLED/INTERVIEW_RECORDING_ENABLED and the new real GEMINI_API_KEY exist only in .env.local; not pushed to Vercel and not deployed. Interview Lab — including this Hub redesign — remains fully disabled in production until a human does this deliberately.

requirements.description: Server-side entitlement system: atomic quota enforcement for Free (2 sessions) / Pro (15 sessions, 8 audio) / retries, with real adversarial concurrency proof
requirements.evidence: Built the complete canonical server-side entitlement service (src/lib/interviews/entitlements/). The browser displays usage but never decides plan/quota/audio-allowance/retry-allowance. DB migrations 022-025: interview_usage_reservations table + atomic reservation/commit/release RPCs using pg_advisory_xact_lock per (user_id, kind) to serialize concurrent callers — same atomic pattern that previously fixed the AI-quota race (P0-RACE). Three reservation kinds: session (period quota), audio_session (Pro-only sub-pool, every audio reservation also takes a session slot), retry (Pro period pool / Free per-session-one). Reservation lifecycle: reserved → committed (first real answer) → released (pre-answer abandon or narrow analysis-failure refund). All 6 session-modification routes wired: session creation reserves before inserting (rollback on fail), transcript/live-transcript/recording routes commit on first answer, DELETE refunds only pre-answer reservations, retry route reserves from the correct pool and releases-specific on DB race-lost. GET /api/interviews/usage surfaces real used/remaining/reset-date per signed-in user. BUGS FOUND AND FIXED: (1) PostgREST 'function is not unique' ambiguity between 6-param (migration 022) and 7-param (migrations 023/024) overloads — PostgREST raises an error when omitting the new parameter, which was misinterpreted as AUDIO_LIMIT_REACHED for a brand-new Pro user on their first session. Fixed by always passing p_max_concurrent: null explicitly (7-param call unambiguous). Migration 025 saves the DROP of the 6-param orphan locally — see KNOWN GAP below. (2) Misleading error message: audioErr truthy (RPC routing failure) and !audioRes.allowed (genuine quota denial) were conflated — now distinguished with separate, honest messages. (3) Retry race: the non-atomic priorRetryCount SELECT let parallel same-answer retries both pass the quota gate; the DB unique-constraint on (question_id, attempt_number) is the real final arbiter, now correctly surfaced as 409 RETRY_RACE_LOST instead of 500, with releaseSpecificReservation cleaning up the losing reservation without touching any other reservation on the session. test:interview-entitlements 24/24 (deterministic: plan constants, session-type gating, env ceilings, Free calendar-month boundaries, Pro Stripe billing-period math). test:interview-limits 13/13 (real adversarial concurrency: ≤2 Free sessions under 10 parallel creates, correct denial codes; 8th audio slot race with 3-burst after 7 sequential completions; 0 orphaned reservations after parallel burst; 1 Free retry under 5 simultaneous requests, 4 RETRY_RACE_LOST not 500s; slot refund on abandoned session; no refund on answered session). resolvePlanContext() delegates entirely to the canonical isProUser() helper — confirmed same code path used by the rest of Showcase. KNOWN DOCUMENTED GAPS: (a) Migration 025 (DROP 6-param overload) saved locally but not yet applied to the live DB — code workaround active (explicit p_max_concurrent: null) provides complete protection, but the migration should be applied before the code workaround is ever removed. (b) Free retry enforcement is non-atomic across DIFFERENT questions in the same session: the non-atomic priorRetryCount SELECT could let a user fire retries on question-1 and question-2 simultaneously and both pass (each sees count=0 before the other's DB write lands); the DB unique constraint only catches same-question parallel retries. Since Free is limited to 1 retry per session total, this is a real but narrow bypass vector (requires >1 question per session AND simultaneous cross-question retries). Full fix requires an atomic per-session retry RPC, deferred.

requirements.description: Interview Lab cost-model document (growth/interviews/cost-model.md)
requirements.blocker: The mission required a cost-model document with per-action Gemini cost estimates, average Free/Pro monthly costs, margin risk at $15/mo, and daily/monthly global exposure. Not yet written. The per-user entitlement limits now enforce hard session/audio/retry ceilings which bound per-user exposure, but the aggregate cost model and daily/monthly global budget circuit-breaker math are not documented.

requirements.description: Atomic Free-tier retry enforcement across different questions in the same session
requirements.evidence: CODE COMPLETE. IL-17 gap (b) fixed: new migration 027_interview_reserve_retry_atomic.sql adds the atomic interview_reserve_retry(p_user_id, p_session_id, p_period_start, p_period_end, p_session_limit, p_period_limit, p_count_period) RPC, serialized per (user_id, session_id) via pg_advisory_xact_lock, counting retries by SESSION across all questions (Free per-session ceiling = 1) and by period for Pro (pool 30). reserveRetryUsage() in src/lib/interviews/entitlements/reserve.ts now routes BOTH tiers through this RPC and no longer accepts a non-atomic priorRetryCount; the retry route dropped the racy SELECT count(*) over interview_answers and lets the RPC decide allowance atomically. A denial inserts no row (a lost race burns nothing); the returned reservation is released specifically if the answer insert later loses the same-answer 23505 race. test:interview-limits extended with parallel retries across DIFFERENT questions of one Free session (exactly 1 succeeds, no cross-question bypass), clean rejection of the rest, and a no-orphan/no-double-count reservation assertion. typecheck + build + lint all green. NOT YET LIVE: migration 027 not applied to the remote DB from this environment (no usable direct DB connection — .env.local DATABASE_URL is a placeholder; supabase db push is blocked by a desynced remote migration-history table; the live RPC is confirmed absent via PGRST202).
requirements.blocker: Engineering is done and committed; only the credentialed live-DB apply of migration 027 remains (same apply path as MIG025-APPLY): Supabase dashboard SQL editor, or a credentialed psql/db-push. After apply, test:interview-limits runs end-to-end.

requirements.description: Apply migration 025 (DROP 6-param overload) + 027 (atomic retry RPC) to live Supabase DB
requirements.evidence: Attempted to apply migrations 025 and 027 to the live project (yogwhfrjhcbnvoxitcay) but no usable DB write path exists in this environment: psql is not installed, .env.local DATABASE_URL is a placeholder (host 'db.your-project-ref.supabase.co'), no pg/postgres node driver is in the project, no exec_sql RPC exists, and supabase db push is blocked because the remote migration-history table is desynced from the local sequential files (remote has 20 timestamp-named versions absent locally; push refuses without a migration repair / db pull, which is risky DB-history surgery out of scope for this closure). Supabase REST/service-role IS reachable and confirmed migration 027's interview_reserve_retry RPC is NOT yet present remotely (PGRST202), and the migration 025 6-param overload status could not be altered. The code workaround for 025 (always passing the explicit 7th p_max_concurrent param) remains fully active and protective.
requirements.blocker: Apply 025 and 027 via the Supabase dashboard SQL editor (paste both files), or run `supabase migration repair` + `supabase db push` / a credentialed psql, from an environment that holds the real DB password. Until then the IL-17 atomic retry fix is committed but inert in production (reserveRetryUsage will fail-closed with a 503 because the RPC is missing) — so 027 MUST be applied before Interview Lab retry is enabled in prod.
